explanation: Directory List Evasion: The Sigma rule relies on a hardcoded list of suspicious directories (AppData, Temp, Users\Public). Executing the DLL from an unmonitored, commonly writable directory like C:\ProgramData completely bypasses the selection_cli directory match.

Exact String Evasion (Extension Omission): The rule explicitly requires the command line to contain 'shell32.dll'. Because rundll32.exe can automatically resolve and append the .dll extension, passing just 'shell32' causes the 'CommandLine|contains|all' condition to fail.

Path Obfuscation (8.3 Short Names): The rule checks for the literal string '\AppData\'. By utilizing Windows 8.3 short naming conventions (e.g., APPDAT~1 instead of AppData), the literal string match is evaded while still executing from the target directory.
